records.ety: [L. chasma, Gr. cha`sma, fr. chai`nein to gape, to open wide. See Chaos.]

defs: A deep opening made by disruption, as a breach in the earth or a rock; a yawning abyss; a cleft; a fissure.
quotes: That deep, romantic chasm which slanted down the green hill.

defs: A void space; a gap or break, as in ranks of men.
quotes: Memory . . . fills up the chasms of thought.
